evidence: src/cowabunga_api/typedef/common.py defines MetadataObject identically twice (lines 5-13 and 31-39)

packages/vllm/src/main.py lives under a src/ subdirectory while packages/repeater/main.py, packages/whisper/main.py, packages/text-embeddings/main.py, and packages/llama-cpp-python/main.py are all flat at package root
src/cowabunga_api/data/crud_file_bucket.py and crud_vector_content.py do not inherit from CRUDBase and use ad-hoc method names (upload/download/delete vs create/get/list/update/delete)
src/cowabunga_evals/metrics/correctness.py inherits from GEval while annotation_relevancy.py and niah_metrics.py inherit from BaseMetric with custom measure/a_measure implementations
src/cowabunga_api/data/database/turso_auth.py and turso_client.py use @dataclass for User, Session, and TursoResult while the rest of the codebase uses pydantic BaseModel with no documented rationale
src/cowabunga_api/typedef/threads/thread_create.py uses Optional[List[Attachment]] (line 75) while modern pipe-union syntax is used everywhere else
src/cowabunga_api/routers/supabase_session.py defines no APIRouter and is a dependency module placed among router files
src/cowabunga_sdk/llm.py defines a class decorator as def LLM(_cls) using PascalCase instead of snake_case

convention_outlier.dimension_character: The codebase follows strong dominant conventions—snake_case functions, PascalCase classes, pydantic BaseModel for API types, and a consistent CRUD naming pattern—with localized outliers in CRUD protocols, metrics base classes, package layout, and type strategies that create minor friction for developers navigating unfamiliar subsystems.
convention_outlier.score_rationale: A 75.0 reflects solid convention adherence overall with several localized but genuine inconsistencies: a duplicate class definition, one package using src/ while siblings are flat, two CRUD classes bypassing the shared base, mixed dataclass/BaseModel strategies without rationale, and a misplaced dependency module. None are systemic, but together they create enough friction to keep the score in the mid-70s.

issues.summary: MetadataObject class defined twice identically in the same file
evidence: Lines 5-13 define class MetadataObject with __init__ and __getattr__
Lines 31-39 repeat the exact same class MetadataObject definition
issues.suggestion: Remove the duplicate MetadataObject definition at lines 31-39

issues.summary: vllm package uses src/ subdirectory while all sibling packages are flat
evidence: vllm places main.py, config.py, and model_download.py under packages/vllm/src/
repeater, whisper, text-embeddings, and llama-cpp-python place main.py directly at package root
text-embeddings and llama-cpp-python place model_download.py under scripts/ rather than src/
issues.suggestion: Move vllm Python files to package root to match sibling layout, or document the structural deviation

issues.summary: CRUDFileBucket and CRUDVectorContent do not inherit from CRUDBase and use divergent method signatures
evidence: CRUDFileBucket defines its own __init__(db, model) with upload/download/delete instead of create/get/list/update/delete
CRUDVectorContent defines its own __init__(db) with add_vectors/get_vector/delete_vectors/similarity_search instead of inheriting CRUDBase generic methods
Eight sibling CRUD modules (CRUDRun, CRUDThread, CRUDMessage, etc.) consistently inherit from CRUDBase[ModelType]
issues.suggestion: Refactor CRUDFileBucket and CRUDVectorContent to inherit from CRUDBase where feasible, or rename them to avoid the CRUD prefix if they follow a fundamentally different protocol

issues.summary: CorrectnessMetric extends GEval while sibling metrics extend BaseMetric
evidence: correctness.py inherits from deepeval.metrics.GEval and relies on GEval's built-in measure()
annotation_relevancy.py and niah_metrics.py inherit from BaseMetric and implement measure(), a_measure(), is_successful(), and __name__ property manually
issues.suggestion: Make CorrectnessMetric consistent with siblings by extending BaseMetric and implementing the same interface, or add a comment documenting why GEval is intentionally used

issues.summary: Turso database modules use dataclasses while the rest of the codebase uses pydantic BaseModel
evidence: turso_auth.py defines User, UserResponse, and Session as @dataclass
turso_client.py defines TursoResult as @dataclass
All API typedef modules and the database base config use pydantic BaseModel with no documented rationale for the dataclass exception
issues.suggestion: Add a comment explaining why dataclasses are preferred for Turso domain objects, or migrate them to pydantic BaseModel for consistency

issues.summary: thread_create.py uses Optional[List[...]] instead of modern pipe-union syntax
evidence: Line 75: attachments: Optional[List[Attachment]]
The rest of the codebase consistently uses pipe unions such as list[Message] | None and dict | None

issues.summary: supabase_session.py is a dependency module placed in the routers directory
evidence: supabase_session.py defines Session = Annotated[AsyncClient, Depends(...)] and auth helper functions but does not declare an APIRouter
All other top-level files in routers/ (base.py, health.py) and all subdirectories (cowabunga/, openai/) define APIRouter instances and route handlers
issues.suggestion: Move supabase_session.py to src/cowabunga_api/dependencies/ or src/cowabunga_api/utils/ to match its actual role

issues.summary: LLM class decorator uses PascalCase instead of snake_case
evidence: llm.py defines 'def LLM(_cls):' used as @LLM decorator on Model classes
Python convention uses snake_case for function decorators (e.g., @classmethod, @property, @lru_cache)
issues.suggestion: Rename LLM to llm_decorator or apply_llm to follow Python naming conventions

root_causes: Multiple independent backend implementations (Supabase vs Turso, vllm vs repeater vs whisper) were added at different times without a shared style guide
CRUDBase was introduced after some CRUD modules were already written ad-hoc, leaving legacy shapes in place
possible_false_positives: PascalCase method names in packages/repeater/main.py, packages/whisper/main.py, and cowabunga_sdk/llm.py are framework-required gRPC servicer overrides, not naming drift

add.header: Pydantic BaseModel as dominant type strategy
add.description: Nearly all API request/response types and database config classes use pydantic BaseModel. This is a settled, positive convention that keeps serialization and validation behavior consistent across the codebase.

add.header: CRUD prefix convention for data access classes
add.description: The data layer consistently names classes with a CRUD prefix (e.g., CRUDRun, CRUDThread). Most inherit from CRUDBase[ModelType], establishing a predictable pattern for database operations.

add.header: gRPC servicer PascalCase methods are framework-required
add.description: Methods like ChatComplete, CountTokens, CreateEmbedding, Transcribe, and Translate use PascalCase because they override generated gRPC servicer stubs. This is intentional and forced by the protobuf interface, not a naming drift.

add.header: Modern pipe-union typing syntax is codebase standard
add.description: The codebase overwhelmingly uses X | None instead of Optional[X] and list[X] instead of List[X]. Legacy typing imports are outliers.
